selection.caveat: window boundaries derive from LLM-generated timestamps; frames are canonical
transcript_excerpt: [17s-27s] They place the new stator onto the machine's central fixture, adjusting it until it is securely mounted. They then pull the copper wire from the feed, thread it, and position it on the stator to prepare for the next winding sequence.

[27s-32s] The machine engages and automatically winds the wire into the stator. The person keeps both hands close to the rotating component, lightly touching the stator and guiding the wire to ensure it feeds smoothly and aligns correctly.

[32s-38s] The machine stops. The person removes the stator from the mount, turns, and walks over to the metal table. They stand idle for a few seconds holding the stator, looking around the workshop area.
[38s-43s] They turn back around, return to the machine, and remount the exact same stator back onto the central fixture. They grab the wire feed and position their hands to resume working.

[43s-66s] The person resumes the manual winding process. They pull large loops of copper wire with their left hand, twist them, and use the small metal tool in their right hand to pack the wire tightly into specific slots of the stator. They continually adjust the rotation of the stator with their hands as they repeat these looping and packing motions.
window_activities: slide stator core onto winding machine shaft
thread copper wire into stator fixture
